_doc: Stage 04 cumulative discovery input contract. After the orchestration rework, the adapter and guard logic still starts in Query Journey, but the same contract now flows directly into Stage 05 execution and Stage 06 SERP processing inside searchDiscovery.js. Updated 2026-03-18.

input.queries: Schema 4 handoff rows with q, query_hash, family, group_key, target_fields, preferred_domains, and exact_match_required

output.queryRows: Array<RowObject> - source='schema4_planner', domain_hint from the first preferred_domain, doc_hint=''

input.rows: adapter output rows or fallback query rows
input.variantGuardTerms: optional guard aliases from Search Profile

output.rows: filtered rows that still point at the locked product
output.rejectLog: Array<{ query, source, reason, stage:'pre_execution_guard', detail }>

input.config: searchEngines, discoveryInternalFirst, discoveryInternalMinResults, discoveryResultsPerQuery, discoveryQueryConcurrency, and provider settings
input.frontierDb: query cooldown cache and query recording
input.categoryConfig: approved hosts, source roles, and field order

output.rawResults: provider rows from internal corpus, frontier reuse, internet providers, or plan-only fallback

input.searchProfileKeys: storage keys for input/run/latest profile artifacts
input.effectiveHostPlan: optional host-plan policy used by the fallback branch
input.focusGroups: NeedSet pressure signals used for lane quotas
input.learning: yield data and search memory used during scoring

subphases: applyHardDropFilter() rejects protocol, denylist, and cooldown failures before semantic scoring
classifyUrlCandidate() derives host, rootDomain, tier, role, doc_kind_guess, identity_match_level, variant_guard_hit, and multi_model_hint
assignSoftLabels() keeps semantic edge cases as annotations instead of immediate drops
assignLanes(), computeLaneQuotas(), and selectByLaneQuota() choose the selected set
rerankSerpResults() may reorder the selected set when deterministic quality is insufficient and routed plan access exists
writeSearchProfileArtifacts() persists the executed search_profile and serp_explorer
